elements.sent-2411-001: Given an abelian variety $A$ over a global function field $K$ of characteristic $p>0$ and an irreducible complex continuous representation $\psi$ of the absolute Galois group of $K$, we obtain a BSD-type formula for the leading term of Hasse--Weil--Artin $L$-function for $(A,\psi)$ at $s=1$ under certain technical hypotheses.
elements.sent-2411-002: The formula we obtain can be applied quite generally; for example, it can be applied to the $p$-part of the leading term even when $\psi$ is weakly wildly ramified at some place under additional hypotheses.
elements.sent-2411-003: Our result is the function field analogue of the work of D.~Burns and D.~Macias~Castillo \cite{BurnsMaciasCastillo:EquivBSD , built upon the work on the equivariant refinement of the BSD conjecture by D.Burns, M.~Kakde and the first-named author \cite{BurnsKakdeKim:EquivBSDTame .}}

elements.sent-2411-005: In \cite{Tate:BourbakiBSD , Tate gave a uniform formulation of the conjecture of Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer (or the BSD conjecture) for abelian varieties over global fields of any characteristic.}
